title: Live markets: Bitcoin drops to $62,000, stocks slide, oil soars as Iran ceasefire collapses
content: Hedge funds have turned the most bearish on the yen since 2007, boosting bets on further losses to nearly 138,000 contracts as of June 30.
summary: Japan's significantly weakening yen is reportedly pushing companies to invest in Bitcoin and XRP as a hedge against currency depreciation. Hedge funds are increasingly bearish on the yen, making record bets on its further decline, which highlights the perceived utility of cryptocurrencies in this economic climate.

title: StarkWare CEO suggests 4% annual Bitcoin inflation to replace 21M cap
content: StarkWare CEO Eli Ben-Sasson argued that Bitcoin private keys get lost over time, meaning the amount of usable Bitcoin will diminish. Many disagree.
summary: StarkWare CEO Eli Ben-Sasson proposed a controversial 4% annual Bitcoin inflation to counteract the diminishing supply caused by lost private keys. This suggestion challenges Bitcoin's fundamental 21 million supply cap, a core tenet for many in the crypto community.

title: Crypto VC Paradigm launches $1.2 billion AI fund as it broadens beyond digital assets
content: The firm's latest fund backs AI and robotics startups but leadership says it remains committed to crypto investing.
summary: Paradigm, a crypto venture capital firm, has launched a $1.2 billion fund to invest in AI and robotics startups, while maintaining its commitment to crypto investing. This move marks a significant expansion of the firm's focus beyond digital assets. The fund will back startups in the AI and robotics space.

title: Dinari, tZERO join forces on turnkey platform for tokenized U.S. equities
content: The move comes as firms race to build the infrastructure for blockchain-based stocks and debate how tokenized stocks should work.
summary: Dinari and tZERO are collaborating on a platform for tokenized U.S. equities, aiming to build infrastructure for blockchain-based stocks. This move is part of a larger effort by firms to develop the necessary systems for tokenized stocks. The partnership may help shape the future of tokenized stocks.

title: BNB Chain is building a new layer-1 for high-frequency trading and AI agents
content: The network aims to process over 100,000 transactions per second by streaming them directly, eliminating public queues to make trades faster and more secure.
summary: BNB Chain is developing a new layer-1 blockchain specifically engineered for high-frequency trading and AI agents. This network aims to process over 100,000 transactions per second by streaming them directly and eliminating public queues, thereby enhancing trade speed and security.

title: Bitcoin's inflation quagmire gets stickier as renewed MidEast conflict sends oil price soaring
content: Your day-ahead look for July 8, 2026
summary: Renewed conflict in the Middle East has led to a surge in oil prices, which may impact Bitcoin's inflation. This development could have significant implications for the cryptocurrency market. The situation is being closely monitored by investors and analysts.

title: Reserve Bank of India still favors crypto prohibition to curtail tax evasion: Reuters
content: Indian regulators maintained a hawkish stance despite the growing embrace of digital assets and technology by governments worldwide.
summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) continues to advocate for a prohibition on cryptocurrencies, citing concerns about tax evasion. This hawkish stance contrasts with the growing global adoption of digital assets by other governments.

title: Crypto exchange Kraken is trying to become a bank in Europe
content: Kraken's focus is on Lithuania as the jurisdiction to secure the license, according to a person familiar with the plans.
summary: Kraken, a crypto exchange, is attempting to obtain a banking license in Lithuania, aiming to become a bank in Europe. This move would allow Kraken to expand its services and operate under stricter regulations. The license would be a significant step for the company's growth in the region.

title: U.S. SEC to propose crypto rule as soon as this month to ease startups, fundraising
content: A newly updated agenda from the securities regulator shows so-called Reg Crypto high on its near-term list.
summary: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) is reportedly planning to propose a new "Reg Crypto" rule as early as this month. This upcoming regulation aims to ease the process for crypto startups and facilitate fundraising within the industry.

title: Vanguard opens search for digital assets leader in sign of evolving crypto strategy
content: The new role would oversee tokenization, stablecoins and blockchain initiatives as Vanguard reassesses digital assets.
summary: Vanguard, a major investment firm, is actively searching for a new digital assets leader to oversee tokenization, stablecoins, and blockchain initiatives. This recruitment signifies an evolving crypto strategy as the company reassesses its engagement with digital assets.

title: AI trade loses steam as infrastructure boom faces reality check
content: Record earnings, rising competition and concerns over slowing AI spending are forcing investors to reassess one of the market's biggest trades.
summary: Investors are re-evaluating the 'AI trade' as its momentum slows down, facing a reality check. This reassessment is prompted by factors such as record earnings, intensifying competition, and growing concerns over a potential slowdown in AI spending.

title: EDX Markets raises $76 million in funding round led by SBI Holdings
content: EDX’s model targets institutional clients by separating trading from custody through a central clearinghouse to minimize counterparty risk.
summary: EDX Markets successfully raised $76 million in a funding round led by SBI Holdings. The platform aims to serve institutional clients by separating trading from custody through a central clearinghouse, thereby minimizing counterparty risk.

title: Bitcoin, XRP draw Japanese firms as weak yen drives treasury diversification
content: SBI VC Trade says corporate demand for crypto is rising as a weak yen pushes firms to diversify reserves, part of a run that took its registered accounts past 2 million.
summary: Japanese companies are increasingly investing in cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin and XRP to diversify their treasury reserves, a trend spurred by the weak yen. SBI VC Trade reports rising corporate demand for crypto assets, leading its registered accounts past 2 million.

title: Coinbase secures UK authorization to offer traditional investments alongside crypto
content: The license allows institutional traders to access perpetual futures and gives U.K. retail customers the ability to trade equities to begin with.
summary: Coinbase has secured UK authorization to offer traditional investments, including equities for retail customers and perpetual futures for institutional traders, alongside its existing crypto services. This license significantly expands its regulated offerings in the UK market.

title: Bull Bitcoin asks French court to strike down DAC8 implementing decree
content: The non-custodial Bitcoin exchange petitioned to annul a French decree implementing DAC8, arguing the rules could create surveillance and physical risks for up to 135 million European crypto holders.
summary: Bull Bitcoin is petitioning a French court to annul a decree implementing DAC8, citing concerns that the rules could lead to surveillance and physical risks for European crypto holders. The exchange argues that the rules could affect up to 135 million people. The case has significant implications for crypto regulations in Europe.

title: The 5 types of real world assets being tokenized fastest onchain
content: Tokenized Real World Assets span treasuries, real estate, stocks, commodities and private credit. The sector is still small in TradFi terms but it's growing very, very fast.
summary: The tokenization of real-world assets is growing rapidly, with five main types of assets being tokenized: treasuries, real estate, stocks, commodities, and private credit. This sector is still relatively small compared to traditional finance, but its growth rate is significant. Tokenization allows for greater accessibility and efficiency in managing these assets on blockchain.

title: Kazakhstan president signs decree to accelerate crypto adoption
content: Kazakhstan targets gas-powered electricity for mining, income tax exemptions for regulated crypto transactions and cross-border stablecoin payments in a new decree signed by the president.
summary: Kazakhstan's president has signed a decree to boost crypto adoption, aiming to utilize gas-powered electricity for mining and offering tax exemptions for regulated transactions. The decree also supports cross-border stablecoin payments. This move is expected to promote the country's crypto industry.

title: BTC speculators in focus as analysis says 'textbook Bitcoin bottom' is underway
content: Analysis flagged a Bitcoin moving average derivative that last triggered at the end of the 2022 bear market as BTC price action returned to its reversal zone.
summary: Bitcoin price action has returned to its reversal zone, with a moving average derivative triggering, which last occurred at the end of the 2022 bear market. This has led to speculation that a textbook Bitcoin bottom may be underway. The trigger is being closely watched by BTC speculators.

title: Mobile app Toss and blockchain Optimism to conduct Korean won stablecoin POC: report
content: Toss has reportedly partnered with Optimism and Sunnyside Labs for a proof of concept on the feasibility of a Korean won-based stablecoin for payments.
summary: Toss, a mobile app, has partnered with Optimism and Sunnyside Labs to conduct a proof of concept for a Korean won-based stablecoin for payments. This partnership aims to test the feasibility of using a stablecoin for transactions. The project could potentially revolutionize payments in Korea.

title: ESMA turns spotlight on crypto custody risks after MiCA transition
content: The EU securities regulator will assess custody providers’ key management, incident response and reliance on third-party technology providers.
summary: The European Securities and Markets Authority (ESMA) is set to assess crypto custody risks in the wake of the MiCA transition. The EU securities regulator will scrutinize providers' key management, incident response capabilities, and reliance on third-party technology.

title: Secret Network cites AI exploit risks in proposed Arbitrum move
content: “The security risk is the part we take most seriously,” the team said, citing old code and AI exploit risks.
summary: Secret Network is considering a move to Arbitrum but has raised serious security concerns, citing risks from old code and potential AI exploits. This highlights a growing awareness within the blockchain community of emerging threats posed by artificial intelligence.

title: CFTC charges commodity, crypto pool operator with $14M fraud
content: The CFTC has launched a rare crypto-related enforcement action against a commodity pool operator that allegedly defrauded investors of more than $14 million.
summary: The CFTC has launched an enforcement action against a commodity pool operator for allegedly defrauding investors of over $14 million in a crypto-related scheme. The operator is accused of fraudulent activities, highlighting regulatory concerns in the crypto space. This action is part of the CFTC's efforts to protect investors and maintain market integrity.

title: Strike launches ‘volatility-proof’ Bitcoin loans amid bear market, but at a cost
content: The cost of eliminating margin calls and forced liquidations is an interest rate as high as 14.2% and an obligation to pay on time, Strike CEO Jack Mallers said.
summary: Strike has launched Bitcoin loans that are 'volatility-proof', eliminating margin calls and forced liquidations, but with high interest rates of up to 14.2%. This product is designed to protect users during bear markets. However, borrowers must pay on time to avoid issues.

title: SEC crypto rule changes are high on its 2026 agenda
content: The financial regulator’s agenda included proposed rule changes related to crypto broker-dealers, digital assets on national securities exchanges and potential safe harbors.
summary: The SEC has included proposed rule changes for crypto broker-dealers, digital assets on national securities exchanges, and potential safe harbors on its 2026 agenda. This signals significant upcoming regulatory developments for the cryptocurrency industry.

title: Ether climbs toward $2K as Bitmine buys ETH, Robinhood L2 boost
content: ETH charts a path toward $2,000 as TradFi adoption, DAT buying and a long-awaited network upgrade boost investor interest.
summary: Ether is approaching $2,000 due to increased investor interest from traditional finance adoption, DAT buying, and a network upgrade. This boost is also supported by companies like Bitmine buying ETH and Robinhood's layer 2 upgrade. The upgrade and investment activity are driving Ether's price upward.

title: Vanguard seeks digital assets chief after years of crypto skepticism
content: The asset manager is hiring a head of digital assets to lead its strategy for tokenization, stablecoins, blockchain infrastructure and client-facing products.
summary: Vanguard, a major asset manager, is hiring a head of digital assets to lead its strategy for tokenization, stablecoins, and blockchain infrastructure, marking a shift from its previous crypto skepticism. This move indicates the company's growing interest in digital assets. The new role will focus on developing client-facing products.

title: New Hampshire lawmakers to hold hearing on $100M Bitcoin bonds
content: The proposed bonds to be backed by cryptocurrency still need approval from New Hampshire Governor Kelly Ayotte and the state’s five-member executive council.
summary: New Hampshire lawmakers are considering a proposal for $100M Bitcoin bonds, which would be backed by cryptocurrency and require approval from the governor and executive council. The proposal is still in its early stages. If approved, it could be a significant development for cryptocurrency adoption.

title: Kraken wins $22M arbitration against former auditor Mazars
content: The crypto exchange's parent company says Mazars' withdrawal from its 2022 audit caused millions in damages and tied the dispute to Operation Chokepoint 2.0.
summary: Kraken's parent company has won a $22M arbitration against former auditor Mazars, citing the auditor's withdrawal from its 2022 audit caused millions in damages, allegedly linked to Operation Chokepoint 2.0. The dispute highlights the challenges crypto exchanges face in securing auditors. The outcome may have implications for the broader crypto industry.

title: USDT wins payments, USDC wins DeFi as stablecoins diverge: Dune
content: Dune data shows Tether's USDT has become crypto’s dominant payments stablecoin while Circle's USDC powers DeFi, highlighting how blockchain choice shapes stablecoin use.
summary: According to Dune data, Tether's USDT is leading in payments, while Circle's USDC is dominating the DeFi space, showing the divergence in stablecoin usage based on blockchain choice. This highlights the distinct use cases for different stablecoins. USDT and USDC are used in different areas of the crypto ecosystem.

title: Tether invests in Mercado Bitcoin as blockchain finance expands in Latin America
content: Mercado Bitcoin will use the funding to expand tokenized finance across Latin America, adding to Tether's growing portfolio of infrastructure investments.
summary: Tether has invested in Mercado Bitcoin, a leading Latin American crypto exchange. The funding will be utilized by Mercado Bitcoin to expand tokenized finance solutions across the region, further diversifying Tether's growing portfolio of infrastructure investments.

title: Bitcoin Covenants part 3: SIGHASH_ANYPREVOUT
content: APO lets a Bitcoin signature authorize any compatible UTXO rather than one fixed outpoint. It allows for rebindable pre-signed transactions for Lightning, vaults, and layer-2 protocols without new key management overhead.
summary: Bitcoin's SIGHASH_ANYPREVOUT feature allows signatures to authorize any compatible UTXO, enabling rebindable pre-signed transactions for various protocols. This feature enhances flexibility and reduces key management overhead. It has implications for Lightning, vaults, and layer-2 protocols.

title: European Commission looks to expand MiCA to cover emergence of tokenization, non-EU stablecoin issuers: report
content: The European Commission is reportedly seeking comment from stakeholders on expanding MiCA rules until Sept. 30.
summary: The European Commission is seeking comments on expanding MiCA rules to cover tokenization and non-EU stablecoin issuers until September 30. This move aims to regulate the emerging trends in the crypto space. The expansion is expected to have significant implications for the European crypto market.

title: Cardano founding entity EMURGO steps down from Pentad governance role after wallet exploit
content: The exploit, which leveraged a flaw in the wallet's address generation system, drained roughly 16 million ADA worth about $2.4 million.
summary: Cardano's founding entity EMURGO has stepped down from its governance role in Pentad after a wallet exploit drained $2.4 million in ADA, leveraging a flaw in the wallet's address generation system. The incident highlights security concerns in cryptocurrency wallets. EMURGO's decision comes amidst the fallout from the exploit.

title: BNB Chain builds new Layer 1 for agentic trading, targets 2027 mainnet
content: BNB Chain is building a new Layer 1 for agentic trading with sub-50ms preconfirmation and no public mempool, targeting testnet by end-2026.
summary: BNB Chain is developing a new Layer 1 blockchain for agentic trading, aiming for a testnet release by the end of 2026 and a mainnet launch in 2027. The new blockchain promises sub-50ms preconfirmation and no public mempool. This could significantly impact the cryptocurrency trading landscape.

title: India central bank seeks to bar financial institutions from exposure to crypto assets: Reuters
content: India's central bank called for banks to be barred from crypto exposure as it reiterated support for tighter restrictions on digital assets.
summary: India's central bank is seeking to prevent financial institutions from having exposure to crypto assets, calling for tighter restrictions on digital assets. This move is part of the bank's efforts to regulate the use of cryptocurrencies in the country. The bank has reiterated its support for stricter rules on digital assets.

title: Tom Lee’s Bitmine adds $70 million worth of ETH to treasury: onchain analyst
content: Bitmine's most recent disclosure shows that it holds 5.74 million ETH, roughly 4.8% of Ethereum's circulating supply.
summary: Bitmine, a company founded by Tom Lee, has added $70 million worth of ETH to its treasury, now holding 5.74 million ETH, approximately 4.8% of Ethereum's circulating supply. This significant investment indicates a strong vote of confidence in Ethereum. The large holdings could impact the cryptocurrency's market dynamics.

title: CFTC charges North Carolina man over alleged $14 million crypto, futures fraud
content: The CFTC charged a NC man and his company after the agency said he swindled about 60 people out of $14 million in a commodity pool.
summary: The CFTC has charged a North Carolina man and his company with allegedly swindling around 60 people out of $14 million in a commodity pool, involving crypto and futures fraud. The agency claims the man operated a fraudulent scheme. This case highlights regulatory actions against crypto-related fraud.

title: Jack Mallers’ Strike launches ‘volatility-proof’ bitcoin loans built to protect against liquidation
content: Collateral can be partially liquidated, however, if a borrower misses an interest or maturity payment and fails to pay within a grace period.
summary: Strike, founded by Jack Mallers, has introduced 'volatility-proof' bitcoin loans, designed to safeguard borrowers against liquidation. These loans allow for partial liquidation of collateral if a borrower misses payments. The goal is to provide a more secure lending experience for bitcoin users.

title: ZEC jumps 12% as Zcash nears mathematical proof against hidden counterfeit bugs
content: Project Tachyon outlined new details of its verification effort for Zcash's upcoming Ironwood shielded pool.
summary: Zcash's upcoming Ironwood shielded pool is nearing a mathematical proof against hidden counterfeit bugs, thanks to Project Tachyon's verification effort, causing ZEC to jump 12% in value. This development aims to enhance the security of Zcash's blockchain. The verification effort is a significant step towards ensuring the integrity of the network.

title: Ondo Finance says tokenized stocks can now be used as collateral for perp trading
content: Last year, the tokenized real-world asset issuer launched 24/7 onchain access to more than 100 U.S. stocks and ETFs.
summary: Ondo Finance now allows tokenized stocks to be used as collateral for perpetual trading, building on its previous launch of 24/7 on-chain access to over 100 US stocks and ETFs. This move expands the use cases for tokenized assets. The development has implications for both crypto and traditional finance.

title: SEC plans crypto rule changes for exchanges and broker dealers in 2026 regulatory agenda
content: The SEC is laying the groundwork for cryptocurrency rule changes before the end of the year, with a blueprint for exchanges and brokers.
summary: The SEC plans to introduce cryptocurrency rule changes for exchanges and broker dealers in 2026. The regulatory agenda aims to lay the groundwork for these changes before the end of the year. This move may impact the cryptocurrency industry

title: 1kx, Blockchain Capital back $7.5 million KOR Protocol Series A at $100 million valuation
content: KOR Protocol is building an onchain clearinghouse that helps register and route creative works and pay out rights holders.
summary: KOR Protocol has raised $7.5 million in a Series A funding round at a $100 million valuation, backed by 1kx and Blockchain Capital, to develop an on-chain clearinghouse for registering and routing creative works. The protocol aims to facilitate payouts to rights holders. The investment highlights the growing interest in blockchain-based solutions for content ownership and monetization.

title: Tether puts $20 million behind Mercado Bitcoin amid Latin America’s tokenization boom
content: Mercado Bitcoin has grown from an early crypto exchange into a regulated digital financial services platform.
summary: Tether has invested $20 million in Mercado Bitcoin, a digital financial services platform in Latin America, amid a surge in tokenization in the region. Mercado Bitcoin has evolved from a crypto exchange to a regulated platform. This investment reflects the growing significance of Latin America in the global crypto market.

title: Gemini offers 0% stock trading in US as it looks to become ‘all-in-one financial super app’
content: Gemini said it is looking to become an “all-in-one financial super app" as it launches 0% commission stock trading for eligible U.S. users.
summary: Gemini has launched 0% commission stock trading for eligible US users, aiming to become an all-in-one financial super app. This move expands Gemini's services beyond cryptocurrency. The company seeks to provide a comprehensive financial platform.

title: Multi-trillion-dollar asset manager Vanguard hiring crypto exec to help influence market
content: The firm's head of digital assets will lead Vanguard's crypto-related strategy for personal wealth clients and interact with regulators.
summary: Vanguard, a multi-trillion-dollar asset manager, is hiring a crypto executive to lead its digital assets strategy for personal wealth clients and engage with regulators. This move indicates Vanguard's growing interest in the cryptocurrency market. The new hire will help shape the company's crypto-related strategy.

title: Polymarket Turns On Instant Bitcoin Deposits Via Lightning Network, Powered by Spark
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Polymarket Turns On Instant Bitcoin Deposits Via Lightning Network, Powered by Spark
Polymarket has enabled instant self-custodial Bitcoin deposits via the Lightning Network using Spark.
This post Polymarket Turns On Instant Bitcoin Deposits Via Lightning Network, Powered by Spark first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: Polymarket has enabled instant self-custodial Bitcoin deposits via the Lightning Network using Spark, allowing for faster and more efficient transactions. This integration is powered by Spark and is a significant development for the Bitcoin community. It enables users to deposit Bitcoin instantly and securely.

title: Kraken Seeks Final Judgment After $22 Million Award Against Former Auditor
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Kraken Seeks Final Judgment After $22 Million Award Against Former Auditor
Kraken is seeking a final Delaware court judgment against former auditor Mazars after winning a $22 million arbitration award, arguing the firm's abrupt resignation during heightened U.S. regulatory pressure caused significant financial and operational harm.
This post Kraken Seeks Final Judgment After $22 Million Award Against Former Auditor first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: Kraken is seeking a final judgment against its former auditor Mazars after being awarded $22 million in arbitration. The award was granted due to Mazars' abrupt resignation, which caused significant financial and operational harm to Kraken. The resignation occurred during a time of heightened US regulatory pressure.

title: Vanguard Warms to Crypto With Search for Digital Assets Chief
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Vanguard Warms to Crypto With Search for Digital Assets Chief
Vanguard is hiring its first head of digital assets to develop a long-term crypto and blockchain strategy.
This post Vanguard Warms to Crypto With Search for Digital Assets Chief first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: Vanguard is hiring a head of digital assets to develop a long-term crypto and blockchain strategy, marking a significant move into the cryptocurrency space. This new position indicates Vanguard's growing interest in digital assets. The company aims to establish a solid strategy for its crypto and blockchain endeavors.

title: New Hampshire’s $100 Million Bitcoin-Backed Bond Faces Final Vote
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

New Hampshire’s $100 Million Bitcoin-Backed Bond Faces Final Vote
New Hampshire's Executive Council will vote Wednesday on a first-of-its-kind $100 million Bitcoin-backed municipal bond.
This post New Hampshire’s $100 Million Bitcoin-Backed Bond Faces Final Vote first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: New Hampshire's Executive Council is set to vote on a $100 million Bitcoin-backed municipal bond, a first-of-its-kind bond. This bond is unique and could potentially pave the way for similar bonds in the future. The vote is scheduled to take place on Wednesday.

title: SpaceX Nasdaq-100 Entry Brings Bitcoin Exposure to Passive Index Investors
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

SpaceX Nasdaq-100 Entry Brings Bitcoin Exposure to Passive Index Investors
SpaceX joins the Nasdaq-100 today, triggering ~$4.3B in passive inflows to a company holding 18,712 BTC. This creates rules-based institutional demand for Bitcoin through corporate balance sheets.
This post SpaceX Nasdaq-100 Entry Brings Bitcoin Exposure to Passive Index Investors first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Nick Ward.
summary: SpaceX has joined the Nasdaq-100, resulting in approximately $4.3 billion in passive inflows to the company, which holds 18,712 BTC, creating institutional demand for Bitcoin. This move brings Bitcoin exposure to passive index investors. The entry is expected to have a significant impact on Bitcoin's market.

title: Tether Invests $20 Million in Brazil’s Mercado Bitcoin
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Tether Invests $20 Million in Brazil’s Mercado Bitcoin
Tether invested $20 million in Brazil's Mercado Bitcoin to expand regulated blockchain finance, tokenization and stablecoin infrastructure across Latin America.
This post Tether Invests $20 Million in Brazil’s Mercado Bitcoin first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: Tether invested $20 million in Brazil's Mercado Bitcoin to expand regulated blockchain finance and stablecoin infrastructure across Latin America. This investment aims to promote tokenization and stablecoin adoption in the region. The move is expected to boost the growth of the Latin American crypto market.

title: U.S. Bitcoin Reserve Stalls as Treasury and Commerce Vie for Control: Report
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

U.S. Bitcoin Reserve Stalls as Treasury and Commerce Vie for Control: Report
A dispute between the Treasury and Commerce departments over who should oversee the proposed U.S. Strategic Bitcoin Reserve has delayed its rollout more than a year after President Trump ordered its creation, according to a Bloomberg report.
This post U.S. Bitcoin Reserve Stalls as Treasury and Commerce Vie for Control: Report first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: A dispute between the US Treasury and Commerce departments has delayed the rollout of the proposed US Strategic Bitcoin Reserve, which was ordered by President Trump over a year ago. The delay is due to a disagreement over who should oversee the reserve. The rollout has been stalled for over a year.

title: Bitcoin Suisse Wins Abu Dhabi License, Extends Digital Asset Push into the UAE
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Bitcoin Suisse Wins Abu Dhabi License, Extends Digital Asset Push into the UAE
Bitcoin Suisse won regulatory approval in Abu Dhabi, allowing it to offer institutional crypto services across the UAE.
This post Bitcoin Suisse Wins Abu Dhabi License, Extends Digital Asset Push into the UAE first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: Bitcoin Suisse has received regulatory approval in Abu Dhabi, enabling the company to provide institutional crypto services across the UAE. This move marks an extension of Bitcoin Suisse's digital asset push into the region. The approval allows the company to expand its services in the UAE.

title: The Download: your stake in OpenAI, and the Treasury’s AI warning
content: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Your family’s $300 stake in OpenAI Sam Altman’s proposal that Americans should share in the wealth created by AI is back in the spotlight, with reports that he is discussing giving…
summary: OpenAI's CEO Sam Altman proposes that Americans should share in the wealth created by AI, with a potential $300 stake for families. This idea is being discussed, sparking interest in the tech community. The Treasury also issued an AI warning, highlighting the significance of AI's impact.

title: The foundational elements of AI architecture that IT leaders need to scale
content: With the rapid progress of AI capabilities and the move to agentic systems, organizations are expanding their use cases as the technology continues to grow. That constant evolution also introduces risk, leaving IT leaders to wonder which investments will prove valuable even six months into the future. Returning to the foundational elements of AI architecture—the…
summary: The article discusses the rapid progress of AI capabilities and the importance of understanding the foundational elements of AI architecture for IT leaders to scale and make valuable investments. It highlights the evolution of AI and the introduction of risk, emphasizing the need for a solid foundation. The article aims to guide IT leaders in navigating the growing technology.

title: Your family’s $300 stake in OpenAI
content: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. OpenAI CEO Sam Altman’s oft-discussed promise that Americans will share in the wealth AI creates was in the news again last week. On Thursday, the Financial Times reported that Altman is in…

The report suggests that Altman is working on a plan to distribute the wealth generated by AI. This move could have significant implications for the AI community.

title: Prime Intellect raises $130M Series A to help enterprises build their own AI agents
content: The round, led by Radical Ventures, values the two-year old startup at $1 billion.
summary: Prime Intellect, a two-year-old startup, has raised $130M in Series A funding, valuing the company at $1 billion. The funding will help enterprises build their own AI agents. The round was led by Radical Ventures.

title: Hot French startup ZML releases free product to speed inference across lots of AI chips
content: ZML, a hot French AI startup endorsed by Turing Award winner Yann LeCun, has now released ZML/LLMD, software that could make running AI less costly.
summary: ZML, a French AI startup, has released ZML/LLMD, a free software to speed up AI inference across multiple chips, making AI more cost-effective. This software has the potential to reduce costs associated with running AI. The startup is endorsed by Turing Award winner Yann LeCun.

title: AI chip maker SambaNova raises $1B at $11B valuation, 5 months after last mega round
content: AI chip maker SambaNova has raised at an $11 billion valuation months after Intel was rumored to be trying to buy it for about $1.6 billion.
summary: SambaNova, an AI chip maker, has raised $1B at an $11B valuation, just months after Intel was rumored to be considering a $1.6 billion acquisition. This significant funding round underscores the company's rapid growth and valuation increase. The investment highlights the interest in AI-specific hardware solutions.

title: Meta just launched a new AI generator, Muse Image, and users are already pushing back over use of their photos
content: The new image-generating model has numerous use cases, including advertising and decorating, and creator-based opportunities.
summary: Meta has launched Muse Image, a new AI image generator designed for various applications like advertising and content creation. However, the release is already facing user pushback regarding the alleged use of their photos for training the model.

title: Why the rise of open source AI isn’t hurting Anthropic … yet
content: Open source models’ success isn’t coming at the expense of frontier labs. Instead, they each seem to capture two phases of the same life cycle.
summary: The rise of open source AI models is not currently hurting companies like Anthropic, as they appear to serve different phases of the AI life cycle. Open source models and frontier labs like Anthropic are capturing distinct phases, allowing them to coexist. This development may have significant implications for the future of AI research and development.

title: Microsoft joins AI cost-cutting trend by relying more on its own models
content: Microsoft is the latest Silicon Valley giant to cut back on its AI spending.
summary: Microsoft is cutting back on its AI spending by relying more on its own models, joining other Silicon Valley giants in the trend. This move is part of the company's efforts to reduce costs. The decision may impact Microsoft's AI development and innovation.

title: Discord admits AI moderation bug wrongfully banned users over harmless images
content: The company confirmed that the issue had been affecting accounts since May, with an additional 200 users banned over the weekend before its team identified and fixed the problem.
summary: Discord experienced an AI moderation bug that wrongfully banned users for posting harmless images, with the issue affecting accounts since May and an additional 200 users banned over a weekend before the fix. The problem was identified and resolved by Discord's team. The company confirmed the bug's existence and its recent resolution.

title: Savi’s app aims to protect consumers from realistic AI scams like kidnappers demanding ransom
content: The company just raised $7 million in seed funding, and is launching its app for iPhone and Android on Tuesday.
summary: Savi's app aims to protect consumers from AI-powered scams, such as kidnappers demanding ransom, and has raised $7 million in seed funding. The app will be available on iPhone and Android. This development is significant for consumers' safety against AI scams.

title: The first American autonomous ground vehicles are fighting in Ukraine
content: Forterra has deployed more than 100 of its self-driving ATVs in conflict zones in Ukraine.
summary: Forterra has deployed over 100 autonomous ground vehicles in Ukraine, marking the first time American autonomous vehicles are used in combat. These vehicles are being used to support Ukrainian forces. The deployment showcases the increasing use of AI in warfare.

title: The ‘first’ AI-run ransomware attack still needed a human
content: An AI agent carried out the technical execution of a real-world ransomware attack for the first known time, but new details show a human still chose the victim, set up the infrastructure, and supplied stolen credentials — meaning it wasn't quite the fully autonomous cybercrime debut that last week's headlines suggested.
summary: A recent ransomware attack was carried out with the help of an AI agent, but it still required human intervention to choose the victim and set up the infrastructure. The AI agent was used for the technical execution of the attack. This incident marks the first known time an AI has been used in a real-world ransomware attack.

title: US investors will soon get access to SK Hynix, another memory maker riding the AI boom
content: SK Hynix is experiencing a boom credited to AI. It will ride that to a multibillion-dollar U.S. IPO, expected to take place on Friday.
summary: SK Hynix, a memory maker, is experiencing a boom due to AI and will soon have a multibillion-dollar US IPO. The IPO is expected to take place on Friday. This move will give US investors access to the company.

title: You can now customize Siri’s pace and expressivity in the latest iOS 27 beta
content: The update is part of Apple's broader effort to make Siri feel more natural and personal, as it rebuilds the assistant around generative AI.
summary: Apple's latest iOS 27 beta update allows users to customize Siri's pace and expressivity, aiming to make the assistant feel more natural and personal. This update is part of Apple's effort to rebuild Siri around generative AI. The goal is to enhance the user experience with a more personalized and natural-feeling assistant.

title: Every major tech layoff in 2026 that has name-checked AI
content: A running look — in reverse chronological order — at the bigger tech companies that have announced significant layoffs this year with AI as a stated factor.
summary: Several major tech companies have announced significant layoffs in 2026, citing AI as a contributing factor. This article provides a running list of these companies, focusing on the role of AI in their layoff decisions. The list is presented in reverse chronological order.

title: If you use Google, you’re training its AI. Here’s how to opt out.
content: Consider this a belated PSA: A recent change to Google’s privacy settings is allowing the company to store more of your data, including media such as “images, files, and audio and video recordings,” to improve its AI models.
summary: Google's recent change to its privacy settings allows the company to store more user data to improve its AI models, including images, files, and audio and video recordings. Users can opt out of this data collection. This change affects users who utilize Google's services.

title: Reddit is using LLMs to solve a problem LLMs largely created
content: In the AI era, platforms have no choice but to fight fire with fire to cull spam.
summary: Reddit is utilizing Large Language Models (LLMs) to combat spam, a problem that LLMs have largely contributed to. This approach aims to mitigate the issue by using the same technology that created it. The move highlights the evolving relationship between AI and online platforms.

title: Meta’s new Muse Image model can pull other Instagram users into AI photos
content:
Meta is launching the first AI image generation model made by its Superintelligence Labs division. The Muse Image model now powers the image-making tools across the Meta AI app, Instagram, and WhatsApp, and it's coming soon to Facebook and Messenger, according to an announcement on Tuesday.
It's part of the growing Muse family of AI models that replace Meta's Llama lineup. Alexandr Wang, who Meta hired to head up its Superintelligence Labs last year, says on Threads that Muse Image is "agentic," meaning it works with its Muse Spark large language model "to reason through your prompt, search the web, and plan before it generates." Meta is al …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Meta is launching Muse Image, an AI image generation model that can pull other Instagram users into AI photos, as part of its growing Muse family of AI models. The model is now available across Meta's platforms, including Instagram and WhatsApp. It is described as 'agentic', working with a large language model to reason through prompts and generate images.

title: Anthropic is launching Claude Cowork on mobile and web
content:
Starting Tuesday, Anthropic's Claude Cowork AI platform will be available on mobile and web for the first time. The expanded access is rolling out first to Max subscribers and coming to Claude users on other plans "in the coming weeks." 
Claude Cowork was previously only accessible through the Claude desktop app for macOS and Windows, but now users on iOS and Android can also use it. However, Anthropic says the "full experience" for Cowork will still be on the desktop app, including features like local file access. 
Cowork sessions will also now run in the cloud by default, so you can continue them across different devices or run Cowork ta …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Anthropic's Claude Cowork AI platform is now available on mobile and web, in addition to its desktop app, allowing users to access it across different devices. The expanded access is initially available to Max subscribers, with other plans to follow in the coming weeks. The full experience, including local file access, will still be exclusive to the desktop app.

title: Anthropic wants to develop its own drugs
content:
At the event "The Briefing: AI for Science" earlier this week, Anthropic announced Claude Science, a new "AI workbench for scientists" that pulls fragmented tools and datasets into one environment, and generates figures and visuals. Anthropic, already dominating the industry with its popular coding tools and powerful AI models, framed the launch around what it says is AI's potential to "dramatically accelerate the pace of scientific discovery and the development of healthcare interventions," and touted a long list of biotech and pharma customers already using Claude. 
Anthropic also went a step further, saying it would develop drugs of its  …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Anthropic unveiled Claude Science, an AI workbench that consolidates tools and datasets for scientists, aiming to speed up scientific discovery and drug development, and announced plans to develop its own pharmaceuticals using AI.

title: Hackers can use 9 of the most popular AI tools to assemble massive botnets
content: "HalluSquatting" weaponizes LLMs' inability to say "I don't know."
summary: Hackers can utilize 9 popular AI tools to create massive botnets by exploiting the limitations of large language models, specifically their inability to respond with uncertainty. This vulnerability, known as HalluSquatting, allows attackers to assemble and control botnets. The discovery highlights potential security risks associated with AI tools.

title: Facing US export controls, China's DeepSeek plans to make its own chips
content: It's early, but the plan is to reduce dependency on Nvidia and Huawei.
summary: China's DeepSeek is planning to develop its own chips to reduce dependence on Nvidia and Huawei due to US export controls. This move is still in its early stages. The goal is to decrease reliance on foreign technology.

title: How AI could enable autonomous robot workers in workplaces—and maybe homes
content: Top robotics researchers and founders explain how robot autonomy is evolving.
summary: Top robotics researchers and founders discuss the evolution of robot autonomy, which could lead to autonomous robot workers in workplaces and homes. This development is made possible by advancements in artificial intelligence. The technology has the potential to significantly impact various industries and daily life.

title: Secret Claude tracker shocks users after Anthropic’s anti-surveillance stance
content: Anthropic accused of spying on users; engineer says “experiment” is over.
summary: Anthropic, a company known for its AI technology, has been accused of spying on users through a secret tracker in its Claude tool, contradicting its anti-surveillance stance. An engineer stated that the experiment is now over. This incident raises concerns about user privacy and trust in AI companies.

title: UK regulator warns of "arms race" to keep up with AI use in financial services
content: FCA official makes case for greater powers for watchdog as millions use technology for personal finance decisions.
summary: A UK regulator has warned of an 'arms race' to keep up with the increasing use of AI in financial services, with millions using the technology for personal finance decisions. The regulator is calling for greater powers to oversee the industry. This warning highlights the growing importance of AI in finance.

title: After spooking Trump into safety testing, Anthropic AI models get global release
content: US lifts curbs on Anthropic’s advanced Fable and Mythos models.
summary: The US has lifted restrictions on Anthropic's advanced AI models, Fable and Mythos, permitting their global release. This follows earlier safety testing prompted by concerns raised by former President Trump.

title: South Korea to spend $1T on more memory chip production and humanoid robots
content: South Korea targets physical AI lead and commercial humanoid robots by 2028.
summary: South Korea plans to invest $1 trillion in expanding memory chip manufacturing and developing commercial humanoid robots, aiming to become a global leader in physical AI by 2028.

title: Anthropic says Alibaba must be punished for largest Claude cloning attack
content: Alibaba allegedly used 25,000 accounts to mine Claude over 28.8 million exchanges.
summary: Anthropic claims Alibaba used about 25,000 accounts to repeatedly query its Claude model, resulting in roughly 28.8 million exchanges, effectively cloning the AI. The company is calling for punitive action against Alibaba for the largest known Claude‑cloning attack.

title: Polymarket bets on U.S. marketing blitz to win back trust after 4-year ban: Report
content: The prediction market's head of U.S. operations says the business is taking steps to legitimize itself after years of legal scrutiny.
summary: Polymarket, a prominent prediction market, is launching a U.S. marketing campaign to regain trust and legitimize its operations. This move comes after a four-year ban and years of legal scrutiny.

title: Crypto and stocks tumble after Trump declares ceasefire 'over' following Iran strikes
content: Bitcoin fell, altcoins slid after Trump said the ceasefire with Iran is "over" as U.S. and Iran traded airstrikes.
summary: Cryptocurrencies, including Bitcoin and various altcoins, experienced a market downturn following an escalation of tensions between the U.S. and Iran. This decline occurred after President Trump declared the ceasefire with Iran 'over' amidst an exchange of airstrikes between the two nations.

title: Bitcoin under pressure as Trump says Iran ceasefire is over
content: Major cryptocurrencies are trading in the red as renewed U.S.-Iran airstrikes push oil higher.
summary: Major cryptocurrencies, including Bitcoin, are currently trading in the red due to renewed geopolitical tensions between the U.S. and Iran. These airstrikes are also pushing oil prices higher, indicating market instability.

title: BlackRock-backed Securitize slides 40% after SPAC debut despite tokenization boom
content: The decline fits into a pattern of recently-public digital asset companies sliding after debut, Arca's Jeff Dorman said.
summary: BlackRock-backed Securitize saw its shares drop 40% after its SPAC debut, despite the ongoing tokenization boom. This decline fits a pattern noted by Arca's Jeff Dorman, where recently public digital asset companies often slide post-debut.

title: SpaceX IPO powers record $3.86 billion in tokenized equities trading in June
content: SpaceX tokens captured $1.19 billion, or 31% of total monthly volume, with Backpack’s SPCX leading trading at $1.08 billion.
summary: Tokenized equities trading reached a record $3.86 billion in June, largely driven by SpaceX tokens which accounted for $1.19 billion (31%) of the total volume. Backpack's SPCX token was a significant contributor, leading trading with $1.08 billion.

title: Former Tether investment chief is looking to sell part of his stake in the stablecoin giant: Bloomberg
content: Richard Heathcote is reportedly working with PJT Partners to find a buyer for a small part of his 1.26% holding in the issuer of USDT following his transition to an advisory role.
summary: Richard Heathcote, the former investment chief at Tether, is reportedly seeking to sell a small portion of his 1.26% stake in the stablecoin giant. He is working with PJT Partners to find a buyer for his holdings following his transition to an advisory role.

title: Berachain hard fork to replace dual-token model with WBERA rewards
content: Berachain launched the first stage of its PoL Next upgrade, which will phase out the BGT token and shift the network's reward system to WBERA.
summary: Berachain has launched the first stage of its PoL Next upgrade, a hard fork that will phase out the BGT token. This change marks a shift from the network's dual-token model, replacing it with WBERA as the primary reward system.

title: EU to again vote to extend ‘chat control’ rules
content: So-called “chat control” rules could be revived in the EU, with lawmakers again set to vote on the legislation Thursday.
summary: EU lawmakers are scheduled to vote again on Thursday to potentially revive 'chat control' rules. This legislation aims to extend existing regulations concerning online communications within the EU.

title: Swyftx eyes crypto payments after securing Australian license
content: Swyftx interim co-CEO Andrea Yuen says the company "won’t be a pure crypto spot exchange in future,” after it secured a license allowing it to offer payment services.
summary: Swyftx, an Australian crypto exchange, has secured a license enabling it to offer payment services, signalling a strategic move beyond its current role as a pure crypto spot exchange. This expansion indicates the company's intention to integrate cryptocurrency into broader financial payment solutions.

title: Nigel Farage to resign from UK Parliament amid crypto “gift” scandal, will stand in by-election
content: The Reform party leader and Brexit champion confirmed that he was the subject of probes by UK authorities following reports of what he called “gifts” from figures tied to crypto ventures.
summary: Nigel Farage, leader of the Reform party, is resigning from UK Parliament after being investigated for "gifts" received from figures linked to crypto ventures. He will contest a by-election, with the scandal highlighting the intersection of high-profile politics and the cryptocurrency industry.

title: Institutional crypto exchange EDX lands $76M from SBI Holdings
content: The funding round comes as institutional investors continue backing crypto market infrastructure despite slower venture investment across the digital asset sector.
summary: Institutional crypto exchange EDX has successfully raised $76 million from SBI Holdings. This significant funding round highlights ongoing institutional confidence and investment in crypto market infrastructure, even amidst a broader slowdown in venture capital across the digital asset sector.

title: The fanfiction community is at war with AI — and itself
content:
Fanfiction communities are trying to hunt down writers who haven’t written works with their own hands. | Image: Álvaro Bernis / The Verge	

Over the past week, a new fanworks movement has kicked off, with the aim to root out authors using generative AI. But the detection methods being implemented are questionable, and any fanfic writer could be caught in the crossfire.
Broad distaste around the use of Claude, ChatGPT, and other AI tools has long been a thing in creative communities, including the world of fanfiction. Readers and writers have passed around tips for spotting supposedly AI-generated works, citing anything from em dashes to the broad concept of purple prose. But on June 29th, an anonymous X account called @heatedrivalryai promised a seemingly more reliable solution …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: The fanfiction community has launched a movement to identify and remove works created using generative AI tools like Claude and ChatGPT. This effort stems from a broad dislike of AI-generated content, but the detection methods being used are questionable and risk unfairly targeting human writers. An anonymous X account has emerged, promising a more reliable solution to this conflict within the community.

title: Trump gets OpenAI to offer US 5% stake, far lower than Sanders’ target
content: Insiders say Sam Altman is in active talks with the Trump administration.
summary: Insiders report that Sam Altman is in active talks with the Trump administration about OpenAI offering a 5% stake in the United States, a figure that is much lower than the target suggested by Senator Sanders. The move highlights potential political involvement and investment discussions surrounding the AI firm.

title: Google’s AI buildout drove 37% increase in electricity use in 2025
content: Google tries balancing AI data center emissions with clean energy efforts.
summary: Google's rapid expansion of AI workloads is projected to raise its electricity consumption by 37% in 2025. The company is attempting to offset the resulting emissions by increasing its use of clean energy sources in its data centers.

title: New attack provides one more reason why AI browsers are a bad idea
content: Telling an LLM that 2 + 2 = 5 is enough to make it follow forbidden instructions.
summary: Researchers have demonstrated a new jailbreak attack where simply convincing a large language model that 2 + 2 = 5 causes it to execute prohibited instructions, highlighting another drawback of AI-driven browsers.

title: NYT slams Microsoft for building copyright-infringing supercomputer for OpenAI
content: NYT shifts OpenAI/Microsoft copyright claims after SCOTUS ruling against Sony.
summary: The New York Times criticizes Microsoft for creating a supercomputer for OpenAI that it says infringes copyright, following a Supreme Court decision against Sony. The article highlights concerns over the legality of the hardware used to train large AI models.

title: Notion killing Skiff-influenced email app since most users use AI agents instead
content: Notion is "going all in on using agents to run your inbox."
summary: Notion is shutting down a Skiff-inspired email app, citing that most users now rely on AI agents to manage their inboxes, and will focus on integrating AI-driven agents for email handling.

title: Crypto Long & Short: With MSTR concerns assuaged, look to traditional signals around BTC
content: In this week's Crypto Long & Short, FalconX’s Gaspar Martin writes that BTC is reaching a market bottom and is poised for a turnaround.
summary: FalconX's Gaspar Martin believes BTC is reaching a market bottom and is poised for a turnaround, according to Crypto Long & Short. This potential turnaround could have significant implications for the crypto market. The article suggests looking to traditional signals around BTC for further insight.

title: Adam Back's BSTR scraps SPAC merger, seeks new deal
content: BSTR and Cantor Equity Partners will not complete their merger under the original July 2025 agreement, with the shareholder postponed indefinitely.
summary: Adam Back's Blockchain Technology Services (BSTR) has scrapped its planned merger with Cantor Equity Partners, originally set for July 2025. The shareholder meeting has been postponed indefinitely, and BSTR will now seek a new deal. This development may impact the company's future plans and growth prospects.

title: Citadel abandons multi-year crypto lawsuit to focus on bankruptcy order against an ex-employee
content: After winning a 6 million-pound London arbitration award, Citadel dropped its U.S. trade secrets case, saying another judgment would likely go uncollected.
summary: Citadel has dropped its US trade secrets case after winning a 6 million-pound London arbitration award, citing the likelihood of another judgment going uncollected. The move is part of the company's efforts to focus on a bankruptcy order against a former employee. This decision marks a significant shift in Citadel's legal strategy.

title: SpaceX's first bitcoin wallet movements in six months likely don't signal sales
content: Onchain trackers found the newly public company shifted a tiny amount of bitcoin between its own addresses. Nothing reached an exchange.
summary: SpaceX has moved a small amount of bitcoin between its own addresses, but none was sent to an exchange, suggesting no sales occurred. This is the company's first bitcoin wallet movement in six months. The movement was detected by onchain trackers.

title: Validators embrace XRP Ledger's recent upgrade. But not everyone’s on board yet
content: The new software leads among the network's validators, yet by node count it sits behind the older v3.1.3, and the security amendment bundled with it remains a separate, slower vote. The upgrade needs 80% of the trusted validator list to activate.
summary: The XRP Ledger's latest software upgrade is gaining favor among network validators, though it still trails older versions in overall node count. A separate security amendment bundled with the upgrade requires its own slower voting process. For the upgrade to fully activate, it needs approval from 80% of the trusted validator list.

title: Bitcoin slides as Iran ceasefire collapse sees $75 oil on Hormuz blockade threats
content: Bitcoin price pressure took BTC toward the "crucial" $61,000 mark as oil prices soared on the collapse of the US-Iran ceasefire.
summary: Bitcoin's price has fallen towards $61,000 due to increased oil prices, which soared after the collapse of the US-Iran ceasefire. The ceasefire collapse has led to threats of a blockade in the Hormuz region, causing oil prices to rise to $75. This has put pressure on Bitcoin's price.

title: India crypto tax filings lag trading activity: Report
content: India’s tax department reportedly found that fewer than a quarter of the 645,000 people who made crypto transactions reported them on tax returns.
summary: In India, fewer than a quarter of individuals who made crypto transactions reported them on their tax returns, despite 645,000 people engaging in such activity. This discrepancy has been noted by the country's tax department. The findings highlight a gap between crypto trading activity and tax compliance.

title: Lyn Alden says Bitcoin needs no savior as Strategy sells $216M of BTC
content: Bitcoin-focused macroeconomist Lyn Alden said BTC must stand on its own as Strategy sold 3,588 BTC, while warning about leverage risks tied to STRC.
summary: Lyn Alden, a Bitcoin-focused macroeconomist, stated that Bitcoin must stand on its own, while Strategy sold $216M worth of BTC, approximately 3,588 coins, and warned about leverage risks. This sale and warning come as the crypto market continues to evolve. The sale is significant but the warning about leverage risks is noteworthy for investors.

title: Base to activate B20 standard for stablecoins, RWAs and other tokens
content: Base is scheduled to activate its B20 token standard on Wednesday at 6 pm UTC, allowing developers to begin creating native tokens on the network.
summary: Base is activating its B20 token standard, enabling developers to create native tokens on the network, including stablecoins and other tokens. This activation is scheduled to occur on Wednesday at 6 pm UTC. The move is expected to expand the network's capabilities.

title: Ripple becomes Kansas Jayhawks sports sponsor with XRP patch to appear on jerseys
content: Besides the sports partnership, Ripple has committed to fund financial and technology education programs for student-athletes.
summary: Ripple has partnered with the Kansas Jayhawks as a sports sponsor, with the XRP patch appearing on jerseys, and will also fund financial and technology education programs for student-athletes. This partnership aims to support the education of student-athletes. Ripple's involvement is expected to promote cryptocurrency awareness.

title: Strategy’s 3,588 BTC sale puts future bitcoin selling in focus: analysts
content: Strategy sold 3,588 BTC last week, with CF Benchmarks suggesting continued sales would become a concern if they stop being a choice.
summary: Strategy sold 3,588 BTC, sparking concerns among analysts about potential future sales and their impact on the market. If continued sales are not a choice, it may become a concern. This development puts the focus on potential future bitcoin selling.

title: Nigel Farage to quit UK Parliament, seek new mandate amid probes into crypto-linked gifts
content: Nigel Farage resigned as an MP amid two probes over gifts from Tether investor Christopher Harborne and convicted fraudster George Cottrell.
summary: Nigel Farage is resigning as a UK MP due to investigations into gifts he received from a Tether investor and a convicted fraudster. The probes are related to crypto-linked gifts, sparking controversy around his actions. Farage will seek a new mandate amid these investigations.

title: Kraken wins $22 million arbitration against auditor Mazars for ditching the firm during Operation Choke Point 2.0
content: Payward sued its former auditor, Mazars, for abandoning a nearly complete audit during Operation Choke Point 2.0.
summary: Kraken, also known as Payward, has won a $22 million arbitration against its former auditor Mazars. Mazars had abandoned a nearly complete audit during Operation Choke Point 2.0. The win is a significant outcome for Kraken in its dispute with the auditor.

title: SBI Holdings is sole investor in crypto platform EDX Markets’ $76 million Series C
content: Institutional crypto platform EDX Markets has closed a $76 million Series C funding round, with Japan's SBI Holdings as the sole investor.
summary: EDX Markets, an institutional crypto platform, has secured $76 million in Series C funding from SBI Holdings, a Japanese company. This investment will likely support EDX Markets' growth and development. The funding round was led solely by SBI Holdings.

title: Schwab Strategist Backs Strategy’s STRC Playbook Amid Bitcoin Weakness
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Schwab Strategist Backs Strategy’s STRC Playbook Amid Bitcoin Weakness
Schwab strategist Jim Ferraioli said Strategy's recent preferred stock actions have eased near-term liquidity concerns despite Bitcoin's decline.
This post Schwab Strategist Backs Strategy’s STRC Playbook Amid Bitcoin Weakness first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: Schwab strategist Jim Ferraioli supports Strategy's STRC Playbook, citing eased near-term liquidity concerns despite Bitcoin's decline. This comes as Bitcoin has been experiencing weakness. The strategist's backing is a notable development in the crypto space.

title: Cantor SPAC and Adam Back’s Bitcoin Treasury Renegotiate Merger Terms, Vow New Structure
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Cantor SPAC and Adam Back’s Bitcoin Treasury Renegotiate Merger Terms, Vow New Structure
Cantor-backed SPAC CEPO and Adam Back's Bitcoin Standard Treasury Company scrapped the original terms of their planned merger and will negotiate a revised structure designed to better reflect current market conditions.
This post Cantor SPAC and Adam Back’s Bitcoin Treasury Renegotiate Merger Terms, Vow New Structure first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: Cantor-backed SPAC CEPO and Adam Back's Bitcoin Standard Treasury Company are renegotiating their merger terms due to current market conditions. They will create a new structure that better reflects the market. The original terms of the planned merger have been scrapped.

title: These AI startups are growing  revenue at faster and faster rates
content: There are a lot of fast-growing AI startups, but some are growing even faster, they say.
summary: Several AI startups are experiencing rapid revenue growth, with some accelerating at an even faster pace. This trend highlights the increasing demand for AI solutions. The growth rates of these startups are noteworthy and indicative of the industry's potential.

title: Claude Cowork expands to mobile and web
content: With this update, users can start a task from their desk, get status updates on their phone, and pick up the finished output later — even if their laptop is closed.
summary: Claude Cowork has expanded its services to mobile and web, allowing users to start tasks on their desk, receive updates on their phone, and access finished outputs later. This update enables seamless workflow continuity across different devices. The expansion aims to enhance user experience and productivity.

title: Microsoft lays off nearly 5,000 employees across Xbox, commercial sales
content: Microsoft cut around 4,800 roles, or 2.1% of its global workforce, on Monday — the latest in a series of layoffs that’s stoking fears of AI replacing jobs. The layoffs will hit Xbox and commercial sales the hardest.
summary: Microsoft laid off nearly 5,000 employees, impacting Xbox and commercial sales, amid concerns that AI may replace jobs. The layoffs account for 2.1% of Microsoft's global workforce. This move is part of a larger trend of job cuts in the tech industry.

title: Solos debuts an even lighter version of its camera-less smart glasses
content:
The AirGo A6 are available in multiple designs including several transparent color options. | Image: Solos	

Solos announced a new version of its AirGo smart glasses, one that forgoes cameras for a sleeker design and an AI assistant that relies on voice interactions. Last year's AirGo A5 weighed 36 to 40 grams depending on the frame style, but the new AirGo A6 weigh around 19 grams. Part of the weight savings comes from thinner temple arms housing speakers, batteries, and other electronics. For comparison, the new Meta Glasses announced last month weigh around 54 to nearly 60 grams depending on the style.
Pricing and availability for the new AirGo A6 hasn't been finalized yet, but the smart glasses will support "full prescription lens compatibilit …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Solos has debuted the AirGo A6, a new version of its smart glasses that weighs around 19 grams and features an AI assistant with voice interactions, forgoing cameras for a sleeker design. The AirGo A6 comes in multiple designs and color options, including transparent frames. Pricing and availability have not been finalized yet.

title: I spy
content:
I’m just doing my job, but turns out, it feels lousy holding other people’s privacy in your hands.	

I've long argued that Hollywood has simultaneously set and ruined our expectations for smart glasses. But after binge-watching two seasons of Netflix's A Man on the Inside, this is perhaps the first time I've seen Hollywood, perhaps inadvertently, illustrate the biggest cultural problem with smart glasses as they stand today. 
In a nutshell, Ted Danson plays Charles Nieuwendyk, an elderly widower who finds a new purpose working for a private investigator. Armed with a pair of Ray-Ban Meta-like glasses, a voice recorder, and a smartphone, Nieuwendyk infiltrates a retirement home, and several privacy-infringing hijinks ensue as he hunts for t …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: A Netflix show inadvertently highlights the cultural problem with smart glasses, particularly the invasion of privacy, as a character uses them for surveillance. The show's portrayal raises concerns about the impact of smart glasses on society. The article discusses the implications of this technology.

title: Microsoft is laying off 4,800 employees
content:
Satya Nadella in February 2026. | Photo by Sven Hoppe / picture alliance via Getty Images	

A year after cutting around 9,100 employees, Microsoft is making further layoffs today as it begins its new financial year. The software maker is laying off around 4,800 employees today, approximately 2.1 percent of its workforce. Most of the employees affected by today's cuts are in Microsoft's commercial sales business or the company's Xbox division.
In an internal memo to employees, Amy Coleman, executive vice president and Microsoft's chief people officer, blamed the job losses on a changing technology industry and the "need to adjust resources and roles and shift how we operate" to respond to how AI is impacting companies like Microsof …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Microsoft is laying off 4,800 employees, approximately 2.1% of its workforce, mainly in commercial sales and Xbox divisions, due to a changing tech industry and the impact of AI. The layoffs come a year after cutting 9,100 employees. Microsoft is adjusting resources to respond to AI's impact.

title: Some of the nation’s rich are letting AI teach their kids
content:
Most Americans don't trust AI. It's proven that it doesn't know what safe toppings for pizza are. People don't even want to listen to AI music. But none of that matters for some of America's wealthy, who are turning to AI to teach their kids instead of traditional schools.
Companies like Forge Prep and Alpha School are charging families tens of thousands of dollars to turn their kids into beta testers for AI tutors and "interactive project-based workshops." Unsurprisingly, Silicon Valley have been major adopters of this new model. Shaun Johnson, a San Francisco-based venture capitalist, told The Wall Street Journal that he plans to send his …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Wealthy American families are using AI to teach their kids, with companies like Forge Prep and Alpha School offering AI-powered tutoring and workshops for tens of thousands of dollars. This trend is particularly popular in Silicon Valley, with some venture capitalists embracing the new model. The move reflects a growing interest in AI-based education among the affluent.

title: Infuriating Google commercial imagines the founding fathers embracing AI
content:
I call BS: the founding fathers definitely would have been Microsoft Teams users. | Image: Google	

"Group project, but make it 1776." That's how a new commercial for Google Workspace opens. And things only get cringier from there. The clip imagines what it would be like if the founding fathers turned to Google's collaboration tools and Gemini to help them draft the Declaration of Independence. 
Ben Franklin texts Thomas Jefferson to check on the status of a draft, who takes a photo and uses AI to transcribe it into a Google Doc. Franklin and Adams hop in to make edits in suggestion mode, Gemini finds them a meeting time, takes notes during a Google Meet call, and then Nano Banana whips up a seal for the United States featuring a turkey ( …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: A new Google commercial imagines the founding fathers using Google Workspace and AI to draft the Declaration of Independence, with features like text transcription and virtual meetings. The ad showcases Google's collaboration tools in a humorous and anachronistic way. The commercial highlights the potential of AI and Google's tools to enhance productivity and teamwork.

title: A behind-the-scenes look at Midjourney’s medical scanner leaves many questions unanswered
content:
A scan of an imaging phantom, segmented to validate how cleanly structures separate under controlled conditions. | Image: Midjourney Medical	

Midjourney has shown more of its futuristic medical scanner. It still hasn't shown much proof it works. 
The AI startup, best known for generating images, released a behind-the-scenes video of its dunk-tank ultrasound scanner, which it plans to deploy in spas and hopes will transform medicine with cheap, detailed, radiation-free imaging. The nearly 20-minute tour comes from tech YouTuber Marcin Plaza, who also happens to be an engineer at the company.
Plaza frankly describes the scanner as scores of ultrasound probes "hacked apart and slapped on a glorified hot tub with an elevator in it," connected to off-the-shelf computers and Raspberry …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Midjourney, the AI image‑generation startup, released a behind‑the‑scenes video of its experimental ultrasound scanner, describing it as a collection of hacked‑apart probes attached to a hot‑tub‑like structure with off‑the‑shelf computers and Raspberry Pis. The company claims the device could provide cheap, radiation‑free imaging for spas, but the video shows little concrete evidence of its performance, prompting skepticism about its practicality.

title: Musk’s X poses “serious risk to Americans’ privacy,” advocates warn FTC
content: FTC urged to reject Elon Musk’s bid to end X monitoring amid AI concerns.
summary: Advocates warn that Elon Musk's plan to stop monitoring content on X could pose a serious risk to the privacy of American users, especially given the platform's use of artificial intelligence for data analysis. They urge the FTC to reject the bid, emphasizing the need for stronger privacy protections.

title: Google's new Nano Banana 2 Lite image model is its fastest and cheapest yet
content: They may not look as good, but Nano Banana 2 Lite images only take a few seconds to create.
summary: Google has unveiled the Nano Banana 2 Lite, an image generation model that is the fastest and cheapest in its series. It can create images in just a few seconds, though the visual quality is lower than more advanced models.

title: Trump's plan to redesign every .gov website leads to AI-designed horrors
content: A year in, National Design Studio delays plan to update government web standards.
summary: The National Design Studio has delayed its plan to overhaul .gov website standards originally proposed under Trump, citing challenges that have resulted in AI-generated design proposals that are considered problematic. The delay highlights concerns over the use of AI in public web design.

title: South Korea plans to train entire military as "drone warriors"
content: Half-million-strong military will train on drones as “universal combat tool.”
summary: South Korea announced that its 500,000-strong armed forces will be trained to operate drones as a "universal combat tool." The plan aims to make every soldier a "drone warrior," integrating unmanned aerial systems across all branches of the military.

title: Google finally releases a Finance Android app, promises iOS version later in 2026
content: It took 20 years, but the Finance app arrives just in time to be packed full of AI.
summary: Google has finally launched its long‑awaited Finance app for Android, with an iOS version slated for 2026. The new app is packed with artificial intelligence features to help users manage their finances.

title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
content: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.
summary: The article provides a daily roundup of the latest news affecting the cryptocurrency market, covering topics such as Bitcoin price movements, blockchain developments, DeFi, Web3, and regulatory updates. It serves as a quick guide to current trends and events in the crypto space.

title: Vercel CEO Guillermo Rauch on the fight to split off models from agents
content: "The reality is, when you're optimizing for production, you start looking at a price/performance," Guillermo Rauch tells TechCrunch.
summary: Vercel CEO Guillermo Rauch discusses optimizing production by considering price and performance, which may relate to AI model efficiency. He shares his insights with TechCrunch. The context implies a focus on production and potentially AI applications.

title: Bitcoin bulls battle for $63K as Micron stock eyes 10% drop in US chip sell-off
content: Bitcoin came off two-week highs while US stocks dipped and John Bollinger described BTC price action as "at a critical point."
summary: Bitcoin retreated from two-week highs, mirroring a dip in US stocks, as analyst John Bollinger described BTC's price action as being "at a critical point."

title: Federal judge denies Kalshi’s bid to block New York gambling law enforcement
content: The judge said New York gambling laws, as applied to Kalshi's sports-event contracts, are not preempted by the federal Commodity Exchange Act.
summary: A federal judge has denied Kalshi's bid to block New York gambling law enforcement, ruling that state laws apply to Kalshi's sports-event contracts. The judge stated that these laws are not preempted by the federal Commodity Exchange Act. This decision may impact the use of event contracts in the state.
